description_long: This is a large, multi-tiered pagoda structure with five distinct stories, each separated by wide, overhanging eaves. The roofs are dark brown and slightly upturned at the edges, a hallmark of traditional East Asian architecture. The building is primarily constructed from wood, with visible beams and columns supporting each level, and features a prominent central tower capped with an ornate finial. The base is surrounded by a green platform, suggesting a landscaped setting, and the lower levels have open balconies or walkways. The uppermost story is smaller and more enclosed, with a decorative spire at the peak. The overall design is symmetrical, with intricate detailing along the rooflines and railings.
description: A large, five-story wooden pagoda with upturned eaves, ornate rooflines, and a central spire, set on a green platform.
description_view_0: Front view showing the symmetrical facade, central tower, and wide, overhanging eaves on each tier.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the depth of the structure, the layered roofs, and the offset central tower.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back symmetry, tiered roofs, and the green platform base.
description_view_3: Opposite side view highlighting the stacked stories, upturned eaves, and the prominent spire.
